name: Air Swim
description: Enables the Nightbane to swim through the air the same as if it were water. A Nightbane does not suffer penalties from drying out or breathing air when using this Talent when his Morphus is of an aquatic nature.

enhancement.summary: Maintained after the first minute for 2 P.P.E. per each additional minute.

limitations.otherLimitations: Maximum height above the ground is 15 feet (4.6 m) per level of experience. Speed is the same as Swimming.

name: Air Grab
description: The Nightbane can "grab" somebody up to 50 feet (15.2 m) away +10 feet (3 m) per additional level by making a grabbing gesture in the direction of the target. The grab simply holds a person for two melee actions, or roughly 5 seconds, during which time he cannot move or physically attack.

enhancement.summary: Maintained per grabbing action.

limitations.otherLimitations: Victims must know they are being targeted with this power to avoid it, and even then they need to roll a 16 or higher to dodge.

holdsAndEscapes.combinedPsToBreakEffect: equal to or greater than the Nightbane's

damage.summary: An "air squeeze" does supernatural P.S. punch damage; use the Nightbane's P.S. minus five points and counts as two melee attacks.

name: All-Nighter
description: Staves off the effects of sleep deprivation for an entire day at a time, enabling the user to remain fully awake and completely alert without sleep. Useful on guard duty, when on the run from authorities, or when maintaining a dual identity as an average citizen and resistance fighter. Grants +1 to Perception and +3 to save against sedatives or sleep-inducing powers.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only delays the need for sleep; the character must eventually catch up with at least four hours of rest per day skipped. Can only be activated once per day and may not be used on an unwilling subject. Going more than 3–5 days without sleep feels uncomfortable; after 7 consecutive days awake, roll once per day on the Random Insanity Table (Nightbane RPG, p. 44). Insanities are temporary and disappear after sufficient rest.

ranges.summary: Self or one other by touch.

duration.summary: 24 hours.

save.summary: +3 to save vs sedatives and sleep-inducing powers while active.

notes: Nightbane still need a relatively normal amount of sleep; this Talent postpones deprivation rather than replacing rest.

name: Anti-Arcane
description: When activating this Talent, the Nightbane is surrounded by an aura of black light (easily confused with the Shadow Shield). While this aura is in effect, the Nightbane is utterly invulnerable to the effects of magic! Any magical spell that can be resisted by a save against magic will have no effect on the character. Spells that must be dodged, or have no saving throw, will work normally.

duration.summary: 1 minute (4 melees)

name: Ashes to Ashes
description: The Nightbane must be holding or touching a combustible object to sacrifice, reducing it to ashes in a flash of flames. In the same instant, that fire is directed at a chosen target (line of sight required). Damage depends on the weight category of the sacrificed object.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per weight category of the sacrificed object.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only a single combustible object (generally nothing larger than a love seat) may be sacrificed per use — not a house, porch, fence section, or wall portion. Combustible materials only: wood, paper, rubber, and fabric. Cannot use ceramic, metal, plastic, stone, concrete, magic items, living plants, or living beings. Whole objects only; portions cannot be sacrificed. Damage applies to armor or magical shields first. Fire-resistant targets take half damage; fire-impervious targets take no damage (though clothing may burn).

ranges.summary: Touch for the sacrificed object; 100 feet (30.5 m) for the attack on the target.

duration.summary: Instant.

save.summary: Living targets save vs magic (15+). Failure: full damage. Success: 20% damage (round down). No dodge — the target is suddenly consumed in flames.

notes: Weight to damage: 1–2 lbs = 1D6; 3–5 lbs = 2D6; 6–20 lbs = 4D6; 25–35 lbs = 1D4×10; 40–80 lbs = 2D4×10; 85–120 lbs = 2D6×10. Non-living targets (objects, structures) take full damage with no save.

name: Astral Self
description: The Nightbane becomes an astral being with no physical body. In astral form, the character has all the abilities and limitations of an astral projection, but without a silver cord, or leaving his physical body behind. The S.D.C. of the astral-self is equal to the combined S.D.C. and Hit Points of the Nightbane, times two!

name: Blast Wave
description: Generates a powerful concussive shock wave originating from the Nightbane, expanding outward in all directions or only in front of him. Sends anyone and anything not sufficiently heavy or secured flying several yards away and shatters fragile objects such as windows. Primarily defensive, but can be lethal under the right circumstances (e.g., on a skyscraper roof).

baseActivation.summary: Up to 6 P.P.E. per level of experience (maximum spend per activation)

ppe.notes: Double total P.P.E. spent on activation if used in Facade form.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot selectively affect targets within the radius or forward arc — everyone caught takes damage and is thrown. Exceptions: occupants inside a vehicle, or one to two people in physical contact with the Nightbane who are willed to be protected.

ranges.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius around the Nightbane, or a 40 foot (12.2 m) swath in front; area may be reduced in 3 foot (1 m) increments.

areaOfEffect.summary: 20 foot radius or 40 foot forward swath (adjustable in 3 foot increments).

duration.summary: Instant.

notes: Victims are thrown 1D6 yards, take 2D6 damage, lose initiative, lose two melee attacks, and suffer −5% to all skills for one melee round. Save vs impact (16+) reduces damage by half (penalties unchanged).

name: Bomb-Maker
description: Transforms inert substances into lethal explosives that mimic real-world properties (e.g., olive oil into a Molotov cocktail, sawdust into gunpowder, clay into C-4). Blast radius and damage depend on P.P.E. spent and the amount transformed. Created explosives are indistinguishable from genuine substances, detectable and neutralizable by normal means, and are not magical in nature.

baseActivation.summary: 10 P.P.E. per liter/pound of low-grade explosive; 15 P.P.E. per half pound of high-explosive

activationTiers.summary: Low-grade (TNT, gunpowder): 10 P.P.E. per liter or pound — 4D6 S.D.C., 4 ft blast radius
High-explosive (plastique): 15 P.P.E. per half pound — 1D4×10 S.D.C., 3 ft blast radius

limitations.otherLimitations: Only the explosive substance is created — not containers or detonators. C-4 requires an electronic detonator; grenades and land mines require proper casings supplied separately. Material reverts to its original form when the duration expires.

ranges.summary: Touch to transmute into an explosive material.

duration.summary: 6 hours per level of experience; then reverts to the original material.

notes: Common among Resistance and Warlords Nightbane. Does not deal extra damage to beings vulnerable to magic.

name: Bookworm
description: Devours books, magazines, scrolls, or other written sources whole to instantly know every word — regardless of language. Mundane reading grants total recall as if just read; technical material also grants temporary use of 1–4 related skills at 90% (or boosts existing skills to 90%+1D6%). Spell books or scrolls may temporarily allow casting contained spells if the user can already cast magic.

ppe.notes: 1 P.P.E. per 25 pages of mundane text; 2 P.P.E. per page of a spell book or scroll page equivalent.

limitations.otherLimitations: Any source devoured is utterly destroyed. Devoured text ceases to exist after being eaten; knowledge or abilities become available one melee round (15 seconds) later. Any knowledge gained is not permanent and disappears when the duration lapses. Devouring enchanted or cursed tomes may have unforeseen consequences (G.M. discretion). Cannot cast devoured spells without prior spell-casting ability.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One hour per level of experience.

notes: Diaries and journals may reveal investigation clues. Destroying written knowledge is controversial among Seekers but useful to Nocturne Nightbane in a pinch.

name: Borrow Morphus
description: This rare power allows a Nightbane to assume the Morphus of another of his kind! The character gains the shape, all physical attributes, S.D.C. and Hit Points of the borrowed shape. He can also use any of the Talents possessed by the Nightbane he is imitating, but only abilities that the borrowing character knew the other possessed. The borrowing Nightbane cannot use his own Talents until he resumes his true form.

enhancement.summary: 15 P.P.E. for each additional minute the borrowed Morphus is kept.

duration.summary: 4 melee rounds (1 minute)

name: Brain Freeze
description: Induces severe, debilitating head pain resembling an extreme “brain freeze,” scrambling the target’s thoughts and imposing heavy combat and skill penalties.

enhancement.summary: If the target fails to save, spend 6 P.P.E. at the start of each subsequent melee round to extend duration by one melee round.

ppe.notes: Initial attack includes 1D4 melee round duration on failed save. May extend up to eight additional melee rounds in a row.

cannotAffect: creatures with physiologies radically different from humans (Nightland Hounds, Hunters, Hollow Men, Tarantuloids, etc.)
limitations.otherLimitations: Affects Night Princes, vampires, and Nightbane in Facade form or very human-looking Morphus forms. Victim cannot remember what they were about to say or do unless obvious; if mid-skill, cannot remember the next step until the freeze ends.

ranges.summary: 200 feet (61 m) + 20 feet (6 m) per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: 1D4 melee rounds (15–60 seconds); extendable.

save.summary: Save vs magic (15+, including P.E. bonus). Failure: −5 Perception, −2 all combat rolls, initiative last, −30% to skills, and cannot cast spells, use psionics, or activate Talents until duration ends.

name: Bypass
description: Disables conventional and high-tech locks, alarms, and tied surveillance (cameras, sensors) for padlocks, magnetic seals, deadbolts, handcuffs, car doors, safes, and high-security vault systems. Does not affect magical locks or protections.

activationTiers.summary: Simple lock (diary, cheap jewelry box, file cabinet, bedroom/bathroom door): 1 P.P.E. — instant, one melee action
Standard lock (most houses, padlocks, handcuffs, security doors, car doors/trunks/ignitions): 2 P.P.E. — 1D4 melee rounds
Complex lock (prison cells, hospital wards, commercial security doors, common safes, basic alarms/video): 6 P.P.E. — 1D4 minutes; feed goes dark without tripping alarm
Advanced lock (bank vaults, top-secret data centers, military facilities): 14 P.P.E. — 4D4 minutes

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one lock (and its tied security systems) per activation. Disables locking mechanisms and linked alarms/cameras on that door. Does not work on magic locks or magical protections.

ranges.summary: Touch.

duration.summary: Varies by lock tier (instant to 4D4 minutes).

save.summary: Lock difficulty on D20 (must roll higher): simple 2, standard 5, complex 8, advanced 15 (and alien locks). Failed roll — still locked; try again.

name: Chain Lightning
description: Unleashes a devastating electrical bolt that strikes multiple living targets within a 30 foot radius, jumping about one second apart. Each hop inflicts one die less damage than the prior target. Especially effective against Hounds, Hound Masters, Great Hounds, Hunters, and Darksteel users.

enhancement.summary: Increase initial-target damage by +1D6 per step (up to +7D6 / 10D6 total on the first target). Each hop reduces damage by 1D6.

ppe.notes: 9 P.P.E. base = 3D6 to first target, 2D6 to second, 1D6 to third. Maximum total activation spend is 9 P.P.E. per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only damages living beings — not inanimate objects. Each individual can be struck only once per attack; leftover damage dissipates. Dictating target order beyond the first costs two melee attacks; otherwise hops follow proximity. Hounds/Hunters/Darksteel users save vs 12+ (including P.E.) or become dazed (−initiative, −2 attacks; cumulative). Hunters and other flying creatures take damage using eight-sided dice instead of six-sided.

ranges.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) + 20 feet (6.1 m) per level to targets within a 30 foot (9.1 m) radius between victims (double range and radius in the Nightlands).

areaOfEffect.summary: 30 foot radius between chain targets (60 foot in the Nightlands).

duration.summary: Instant.

notes: Typical attacks start around 6D6 on the first target rather than maximum output.

name: Channel Speed
description: One of a handful of Talents meant for use only in Facade form. Taps into the power of the supernatural Morphus form to temporarily add 6 points to P.P. and 10 points to Spd, plus +1 to initiative and +2 to strike, parry, and dodge (in addition to any bonuses from improved P.P.). While active, the user's eyes dim and wisps of shadow rise like thin smoke from the hands and feet. Even in Facade form, the Nightbane registers as a supernatural being to any beings able to sense such things.

ppe.notes: Eight P.P.E. per two melee rounds (30 seconds) of effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only be used in Facade form. Only the bonuses listed in the talent text are added — do not add bonuses from Morphus features.

ranges.summary: Self only.

duration.summary: Two melee rounds (30 seconds) per activation.

notes: Adds +6 to P.P. attribute and +10 to Spd attribute. Grants +1 to initiative and +2 to strike, parry, and dodge.

name: Channel Strength
description: One of a handful of Talents meant for use only in Facade form. Temporarily adds 10 points to P.S. and 6 to P.E., plus 60 temporary S.D.C. (damage is subtracted from this S.D.C. first). The character also gains +2 to save vs poison and disease and +2 to save vs psionics. The Facade's strength is converted to Supernatural for the duration. While active, the user's eyes dim and wisps of shadow rise like steam from the body, arms, and legs. Even in Facade form, the Nightbane registers as a supernatural being to any beings able to sense such things.

ppe.notes: Nine P.P.E. per two melee rounds (30 seconds) of effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only be used in Facade form. Only the bonuses listed in the talent text are added — do not add bonuses from Morphus features.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Two melee rounds (30 seconds) per activation.

notes: Adds +10 to P.S., +6 to P.E., and +60 temporary S.D.C. Grants +2 to save vs poison/disease and +2 to save vs psionics. Strength becomes Supernatural.

name: Chronosphere
description: Alters the temporal flow in the Nightbane's immediate vicinity. Anyone of the user's choosing within the affected radius must save or move at half normal speed, with half the number of melee attacks (rounded up) and a -2 penalty to all combat rolls against anyone unaffected. Anyone entering the area after activation must save as well. Non-magical projectiles and attacks leveled against those within the field are -4 to strike and do half damage as they slow upon entering the area. Can be used in Facade form without any visual manifestation other than the slowed movement of those affected. The talent user is not affected; those who can sense magic or anomalies in space and time will detect the Chronosphere.

ppe.notes: Fourteen P.P.E. per melee round of effect (21 P.P.E. in Facade form). Cannot be extended, but may be reactivated after the duration expires or it is canceled.

limitations.otherLimitations: Not available until 4th level. The talent user is not affected by the slowing field.

ranges.summary: Five foot (1.5 m) spherical radius per level of experience; double the radius for those with a Morphus feature from the Athlete Table.

areaOfEffect.summary: Five foot (1.5 m) spherical radius per level (doubled for Athlete Table Morphus features).

duration.summary: One melee round per activation.

save.summary: Save vs magic (16+, including P.E. bonus). Failure: half speed, half melee attacks (rounded up), and -2 to all combat rolls against anyone unaffected by Chronosphere.

notes: Targets are chosen by the user. Entrants after activation must save or suffer the slowing effects.

name: Dark Edge
description: Many Nightbane refuse to wield liberated Darkblades lest they be marked as Ba'al sympathizers. Dark Edge temporarily imbues any mundane, non-magical blade with the same abilities as a Darkblade, turning the edge ebony. While active, the blade can parry Darkblades without fear of damage, ignores an opponent's A.R., and inflicts double the weapon's normal damage (e.g. a combat knife that normally does 1D6 would inflict 2D6). An imbued blade may be handed off to a friend or ally to use in combat.

cannotAffect: weapons that are part of a Morphus
limitations.otherLimitations: Non-magical blade weapons only.

ranges.summary: Touch.

duration.summary: Four minutes per level of the talent user.

name: Darkblast
description: An improved Shadow Blast. Unlike Shadow Blast, which is almost instantaneous, Darkblast causes shadows to gather around two arms as the attack charges. Once fully charged, the limbs are brought together to fire a large stream of dark energy that packs even more punch than a Shadow Blast. Alternatively, the blast may be fired from the eyes or mouth (or approximate area thereof). Users are +2 to strike with this attack.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per 2D4 damage dealt, up to a maximum of 16 P.P.E. for a 1D6x10 damage blast.

ppe.notes: Maximum total P.P.E. spent on activation is 3 per level of experience.

rangeMechanics.summary: Fires a large stream up to 1,500 feet (457.2 m).

notes: It takes a few seconds to charge, meaning each Darkblast counts as two melee attacks.

name: Darkmetal Infusion
description: Reinforces a single stationary object (or portion thereof) with the strength of Nightlands Darkmetal. The targeted object blackens and takes on a dull sheen like Darkmetal ore. In addition to strengthening the object against attack, nothing can pierce the barrier and damage anything behind it — even high-power firearms and Darkblades cannot punch through the object's A.R. Only by depleting the object's total S.D.C. can it be breached.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per 100 S.D.C. added to the reinforced object (maximum 500 S.D.C.).

ppe.notes: Total activation spend capped at 8 P.P.E. per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: The object being reinforced cannot be moved in any way or this Talent's effects are immediately negated.

ranges.summary: A 3 foot by 3 foot (1 x 1 m) area per level of experience, up to 3 feet (1 m) away.

areaOfEffect.summary: 3 foot by 3 foot (1 x 1 m) section per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience.

structureReinforcements.targetSummary: A stationary object or portion thereof.

repeatableScaling.summary: Cost is 5 P.P.E. per 100 S.D.C. added, maximum 500 S.D.C.

structureReinforcements.notes: The object cannot be moved or the Talent's effects are immediately negated.

name: Darksong
description: The Nightbane can emit a piercing, deafening sound; it can be an inhuman howl, a mighty roar, an ultrasonic keening, or even electronic feedback, depending on the Nightbane's Morphus. Targets must make a roll to save (P.E. bonuses apply); if they roll under a 10, they are stunned for 1D4 melee rounds.

enhancement.summary: The attacker can raise the number needed to save by expending additional P.P.E.: +1 point per 3 P.P.E.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Stunned for 1D4 melee rounds on a failed save.

save.summary: Save vs magic 10 (or higher if enhanced). Everyone in an AoE blast saves at +4 to resist.

name: Darkwave
description: The Nightbane becomes an engulfing, semi-liquid mass of pure darkness that can engulf and suffocate opponents.

enhancement.summary: Maintained per 30 seconds (two melee rounds).

limitations.otherLimitations: Vulnerable to light, and not advisable in daylight.

rules: Takes half damage from physical attacks directed at the darkness.
Takes double damage from energy and magical attacks from those not trapped inside.

Strong light inflicts damage: no damage from streetlights, 1D6 from a pen light (range: 5 feet/1.5 m), 3D6 from a commercial flashlight (range 10 feet/3 m), 5D6 from a high-powered or police flashlight, and 1D6x10 from a spotlight or magic light or Guardian. (Once per melee round per light wielder).
Cannot be used during the day, or the Nightbane will take 1D6x10 points of damage at the end of every melee round.

name: Darkwhip
description: Creates tendrils of darkness that inflict supernatural damage with a P.S. equal to the amount of P.P.E. spent on creating them. The tendrils can have any shape, not limited to whips and tentacles, although those are the most common. The Darkwhip can be used to strike and also to entangle or disarm, provided those skills are known to the wielder.

enhancement.summary: Additional P.P.E. increases the P.S. of the whip, which increases the amount of damage it inflicts.

duration.summary: 4 melee rounds (one minute)

ppeScaling.summary: Supernatural damage uses a P.S. equal to the P.P.E. spent.

name: Death-Grip
description: Despite the name, this Talent inflicts no damage. It allows the user to hold tight to objects or opponents with an unbreakable grip stronger than any vise. When subduing an opponent, breaking free requires total Supernatural Strength twice that of the talent user; even then, the target and rescuers must beat the Nightbane's P.S. x2. The Nightbane cannot be disarmed of handheld weapons, nor can an object be pulled or pried from his grip. Useful for clinging to moving vehicles, aircraft, flying monsters, or objects to avoid falling. Adds +20% to Climbing and +3 to entangle for the duration.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per melee round of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Even if used on a fragile object or a victim's throat, this grip delivers no damage — but the grip is strong.

ranges.summary: Touch/grasp.

duration.summary: One melee round per activation (extendable by spending 6 P.P.E. each round).

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Target requires Supernatural P.S. twice that of the user to attempt break free; rescuers must beat the Nightbane's P.S. x2. Cannot be disarmed.

name: Dervish Dance
description: The Nightbane can move in a circular pattern and make spinning attacks with incredible speed. The character becomes a blur, as he spins at blurring speeds in a large circle - a circular area 10 feet (3 m) in diameter.

enhancement.summary: Maintained per melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Limited to a 10 foot (3 m) diameter striking range. Must be the Nightbane's first attack of that melee round, and counts as all the character's melee attacks for that round.

damage.summary: All opponents within, or who enter the dance zone gets attacked as if the Nightbane had made an attack roll of 10 + 1 per level of Nightbane (maximum 19). The Nightbane also uses this attack roll number to automatically parry all incoming attacks (except energy attacks).

notes: Instead of a standard modifier, this talent sets an 'as-if' attack roll of 10 + 1 per level (max 19) replacing standard combat rolls.

name: Doorway
description: Creates a door of dark energy that leads to a specific location desired by the creator of it. The Nightbane must have been to the destination point at least once in his life, or he must have line of sight.

activationTiers.name: Nightlands Doorway
activationTiers.effectDescription: Opens a doorway to the Nightlands or back.

rangeOverride.summary: 40 P.P.E. plus 5 P.P.E. per mile (1.6 km) to cross over.

enhancement.summary: 2 P.P.E. spent per mile of distance.

ranges.summary: To a location up to 1 mile (1.6 km) per 2 P.P.E. spent.

activationTime.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) of concentration

name: Down the Drain
description: Primarily an urban escape Talent. Liquefies the user and up to two passengers (plus personal clothing/gear) to travel through plumbing, pipes, or sewer systems. The Nightbane controls movement as liquid — including against water pressure, tide, waves, and gravity. If the duration ends before reaching a place that can accommodate returning to humanoid form, the traveler must automatically reactivate at the usual cost. Movement speed is a constant 1,000 feet (305 m) per minute unless the current is faster. In liquid form, travelers are immune to toxins/poisons and all physical attacks but cannot attack, even with psionics. Filtration systems have no detrimental effect, though the experience is disorienting and unpleasant.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per five minutes in liquefied form. Must reactivate at usual cost if duration ends before reaching a suitable exit.

limitations.otherLimitations: Other than escaping down a drain, moving through plumbing, or extending/deactivating this Talent, a Nightbane in liquid form can take no other actions. Large, heavy objects (large suitcase, duffel bag, equipment) are left behind. Only the user and up to two others plus personal clothing/gear can be transformed.

ranges.summary: Self and up to two others by touch. Must be within 3 feet (0.9 m) of a drain or other open plumbing.

duration.summary: Five minutes per activation (extendable at 6 P.P.E. per five minutes).

save.summary: Unwilling travelers save vs magic (12+). Success resists being transformed and transported in liquid form.

notes: Liquid appearance may vary (motor oil, sludge, sap, blood). All liquefied travelers are -4 on Perception Rolls.

rangeMechanics.summary: Self and up to two others by touch. Must be within 3 feet of a drain.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Movement speed is a constant 1,000 feet per minute unless current is faster.

statusEffects.name: Liquefied Form
statusEffects.summary: Immune to toxins/poisons and all physical attacks, but cannot attack. -4 on Perception Rolls.

name: Dreamer
description: With this power the Nightbane can enter the Dreamstream body and soul. While in the Dreamstream, the Nightbane has a 'psychic S.D.C.' equal to his normal S.D.C. and Hit Points combined.

limitations.otherLimitations: If all psychic S.D.C. are reduced to below zero, the character is slain and vanishes as if he never existed.

name: Dust in the Wind
description: Favored by ancient Nightbane who prize self-preservation. If activated before the character reaches zero Hit Points, instead of falling into a coma or perishing, the user disintegrates into millions of black dust particles whisked away on a magical breeze to a predetermined safe point (up to 2,000 feet/610 m away), then reconstitutes. The character falls into a coma in whatever form they were in when reduced to zero Hit Points until regenerating to full Hit Points and at least half S.D.C. If more than 20% of the dust is prevented from reaching the safe point, the Nightbane remains in dust form for 1 hour per M.E. attribute point, then reconstitutes wherever the dust is — minus an arm or leg and -10 Hit Points for every 10% missing (permanent losses).

limitations.otherLimitations: Before use, a return point or safe haven must be designated. While in dust form the Nightbane is unconscious and mindlessly homes in on that haven at 40 mph (64 kph). Immune to physical attacks in granular form, but any spell, psionic, or Talent that deals enough damage to reduce the dust form below zero Hit Points by more than the character's P.E. attribute kills him.

ranges.summary: Self; dust travels up to 2,000 feet (610 m) to the designated safe point.

duration.summary: Transformation takes two melee attacks (~5–6 seconds). Remains dust in hiding until full Hit Points and half S.D.C. are regenerated.

recoveryStateUntilThreshold.notes: Remains a pile of dust in the hiding place until regeneration thresholds are met.

mobilityBlocks.summary: Dust cloud travels to safe haven at 40 mph (64 kph), up to 2,000 feet (610 m).

notes: Safe haven examples: car trunk/backseat, box truck, under stairs, abandoned building, dumpster, supply closet, parking-structure corner, rooftop, ditch.

name: Earthworks
description: Shapes the ground beneath the user at will — natural rock or dirt, or manmade asphalt or concrete — to create defensive walls, barriers, cover, trenches, or escape tunnels. Attacks that beat a barrier's Natural A.R. penetrate to the other side but strike as wild/blind shots. Offensive uses include rolling earth to knock enemies down (or force Piloting rolls at -20% for vehicles) and crash barriers placed in front of moving vehicles (initiative contest; Piloting -30% to dodge).

activationTiers.summary: Temporary barrier or earth shaping: 5 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: One additional load can be affected per activation at levels 7, 11, and 15.

limitations.otherLimitations: Total area per load is 360 cubic feet (10.2 m³) when shaping earth; doubled if tunneling only (6 ft × 6 ft × 20 ft tunnel). Control and shaping lasts one melee round (15 seconds).

ranges.summary: 5 feet (1.5 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Shaping control lasts one melee round. Walls, barriers, trenches, and tunnels remain 2D4+2 minutes unless made permanent; non-permanent earth returns to its original shape afterward.

rangeMechanics.summary: 5 feet + 5 feet per level. Total volume 360 cubic feet (doubled if tunneling only).

notes: Barrier damage rules vary by material (see book). Penetrating stone/concrete hits on the other side at half damage, -6 to strike, no Called Shot without line of sight.

name: Eavesdrop
description: Hearing becomes equivalent to a parabolic listening device. The Nightbane can focus on one person or a small group and listen from hundreds of feet away while blocking out all but the loudest background noise. Conversations behind a thin wall or window can be heard as if standing in the room. Does not provide language translation.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per minute of use (one minute per 2 P.P.E. expended).

limitations.otherLimitations: At least one target or a specific location (street corner, rooftop, window, etc.) must be visible. Phone conversations may be one-sided unless device audio could be overheard while adjacent to the target.

ranges.summary: 200 feet (61 m) + 50 feet (15.2 m) per level of experience; line of sight to target or location required.

duration.summary: One minute per 2 P.P.E. expended.

rangeMechanics.summary: 200 feet + 50 feet per level; target or location must be visible.

name: Encryption
description: Automatically encrypts any form of communication the Nightbane uses so only intended recipients hear and understand clearly. Written notes appear as symbols; shouted warnings sound like nonsense syllables or a roar to everyone else; surface thoughts are scrambled against Telepathy. Defeated only by magical translation (Tongues or Eyes of Thoth skill) or recording the message and attempting Cryptography at -40%.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per minute of use.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One minute per P.P.E. expended.

name: Fear No Evil
description: Temporarily negates fear before entering conflict with supernatural foes — Nightlords, Night Princes, demonic minions, monstrous enemies, or fellow Nightbane. For the duration, the user is immune to Horror Factor and temporarily negates fear caused by Phobias. Useful for newly inducted Nightbane who have not yet hardened to the war in the shadows.

enhancement.summary: Four P.P.E. per additional minute beyond the base two minutes.

ppe.notes: Base activation grants two minutes (8 melee rounds) of effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: If still engaged when Fear No Evil lapses and Horror Factor was not rolled or was failed, the character must immediately save vs Horror Factor. While active, -2 on all Perception Rolls.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Two minutes (8 melee rounds); extendable at 4 P.P.E. per additional minute.

name: Fire Breather
description: The Nightbane is able to exhale a powerful jet of flames, similar to that of a flamethrower. The gout of fire inflicts 5D6 damage to anything caught in its path, unless stopped by a wall, vehicle or large object. The 'Bane is impervious to his own flames, as well as natural fire and heat when this Talent is active, but takes full damage from magical fire or the breath of other Nightbane.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only be used once per melee round, but counts as an extra melee attack.

combat.rangeSummary: 30 feet (9.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per experience level

name: Flurry
description: Named for the volume of blows it enables. Upon activation, grants preternatural combat speed: +2 attacks per melee round, +2 initiative, +2 to strike, parry, and disarm, +3 to dodge. When directly fighting a Nightlord, Night Prince, Night Princess, Greater Demon, or dark god, gains an additional +1 melee attack and +1 to each of those bonuses.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per melee round Flurry remains in place.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot be used in combination with other Talents that provide bonus attacks (e.g. Fire Breather, Dervish Dance).

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One melee round per 10 P.P.E. spent.

opponentConditionalModifiers.summary: Additional +1 melee attack and +1 to initiative, strike, parry, dodge, and disarm when directly fighting a Nightlord, Night Prince, Night Princess, Greater Demon, or dark god.

name: Form-Lock
description: Neutralizes shape-changing abilities. When a shape-changing being is targeted and fails a save vs magic, it cannot alter its physical form or have it altered by any other means for two minutes (or longer with additional P.P.E.). Applies regardless of whether the ability is natural, magical, or otherwise — vampires cannot mist-shift, Nightlords cannot alter size or appearance (though altering other matter is unaffected), and Nightbane are trapped in their current Facade or Morphus form. Nightbane known to possess Form-Lock may face suspicion from their brethren.

enhancement.summary: Twenty P.P.E. per additional minute beyond the initial two minutes.

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. to Form-Lock one target for two minutes — P.P.E. is spent whether or not the save succeeds.

ranges.summary: Line of sight up to 500 feet (152 m) away.

duration.summary: Two minutes base; extendable at 20 P.P.E. per additional minute.

save.summary: Save vs magic (15+). Failure: cannot alter physical form or have it altered by any means for the duration.

rangeMechanics.summary: Line of sight up to 500 feet (152 m).

name: Frightener
description: For Nightbane who are not naturally terrifying — or who want to be more so. Even those with Awe Factor (such as many Barbies and Kens) see it become Horror Factor for the duration. Manifestation is individual (glowing eyes, black aura, bleeding eyes, deepened shadows, seeming to grow in stature, corpse-like appearance, etc.) but changes are magical illusions created by the Talent. The user cannot select who is affected; allies who know the Nightbane and this Talent are immune unless they must fight or restrain the character.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per +1 bonus to Horror Factor desired, up to a maximum Horror Factor of 15.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot select who is and isn't affected. Allies who know the Nightbane and this Talent are immune to the Horror Factor unless fighting or restraining the character. Maximum Horror Factor while using this Talent is 15.

ranges.summary: Self; added fear affects others who see the character up to 90 feet (27.4 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level.

areaOfEffect.summary: 90 feet (27.4 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level — those who see the character.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds).

name: Ghost Tag
description: Leaves spectral messages (words or images) on surfaces — walls, sidewalks, vehicles, clothing, even people (who must remain still). Visible to Nightbane, Guardians, Astral Travelers, and those who can See the Invisible. Sensitive psychics may read tags with Perception 12+ when looking for them (16+ if unaware). Nightlords, avatars, and Night Princes can also see tags; Hounds and Hunters cannot. Other minions need See the Invisible or a Sensitive psychic actively searching.

enhancement.summary: Two P.P.E. per additional minute to write another 30 words beyond the first 30.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. covers up to 30 words or a recognizable image (~30 words per minute at 4–6 inch lettering).

limitations.otherLimitations: G.M. discretion on message size; ~30 words per minute at 4–6 inch (10–15 cm) lettering is a good guideline. Removed by creator at no P.P.E. cost, or overwritten by another Ghost Tag user.

ranges.summary: Cast upon a surface up to 6 feet (1.8 m) away by writing in the air with a finger.

duration.summary: Up to one week per level of experience unless removed or overwritten.

name: Gunslinger
description: Turns the user into a savant with modern firearms — revolvers, pistols, rifles, assault weapons, rocket launchers, and any projectile-firing gun. The held weapon becomes an extension of the Morphus form, usable with expert precision regardless of prior training. While active: +1 attack per melee with firearms, +3 initiative when drawing/firing, +2 strike and disarm on Aimed/Called Shots (even on the move), +1 strike with bursts, +2 strike and parry using the gun as a melee weapon, no penalty for shooting while running, from vehicles, or unconventional positions. Can "see" through the weapon's sights/barrel for blindfolded or behind-the-back shots. Morphus/body-integrated firearms gain an additional +1 to strike on ranged attacks. Stacks with W.P. skill bonuses.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per melee round Gunslinger remains in place.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one type of firearm receives Gunslinger bonuses at a time (multiple weapons of that type do not multiply bonuses). Other weapons may be used simultaneously but do not gain these bonuses.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One full melee round (15 seconds) per 10 P.P.E. spent.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +1 strike with bursts; +1 strike on ranged attacks when the firearm is part of the Morphus.

name: Heal Facade
description: Enables a Nightbane to extend the superhuman regeneration rate of his Morphus to his Facade for a limited amount of time. When activated, the Facade form heals 1D6+4 S.D.C./Hit Points per melee round.

enhancement.summary: 5 P.P.E. for each subsequent melee round of healing.

limitations.otherLimitations: Duration: One melee round per level of experience.

name: Hotwire
description: Similar to the elite Deus ex Machina Talent. While seated behind the wheel, temporarily takes control of any motorized vehicle: unlock doors/trunk, start/stop without keys, deactivate alarms, operate minor automated systems (door locks, wipers), and pilot even without prior training at 66% + 2% per level. Stunt driving at half normal penalties; +15% to existing piloting skill for the vehicle type while active. Popular with Warlords for car theft; useful to Resistance and Nocturnes for hijacking aircraft, boats, and military vehicles.

ppe.notes: Fifteen minutes of control per level of experience per activation.

limitations.otherLimitations: Motorized vehicles under their own power only; controls must be physically operated (minor electronics manipulated by will). While piloting, the user can only speak — contact broken or forced into combat ends control (one melee action to re-link if Talent still active). Another Hotwire user can usurp control (D20 + M.E. bonus, high roll wins, defender wins ties); Deus ex Machina always wins. Single-pilot vehicles only — larger crewed vehicles limit control to the pilot/helmsman station.

ranges.summary: Touch (must be behind the wheel).

duration.summary: Fifteen minutes per level of experience.

grantedSkills.notes: Base 66% + 2% per level when the character lacks the skill; +15% if already trained in that vehicle type.

name: Hound Leash
description: Temporarily takes control of Hounds, Hunters, and Great Hounds (not Hound Masters), commanding them like trained guard dogs — even against other minions. Starts with one target; an additional Hound may be controlled at levels 6, 9, 12, and 15. If not all targets succumb, the Talent may be used again on remaining targets up to the maximum without releasing those already leashed. Leashed creatures can attack their own kind but cannot be ordered to destroy themselves or attack a Hound Master — doing so instantly breaks the Leash and may turn them on the controller.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per each leashed Hound or Hunter to extend duration one additional minute (no new save).

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per Hound or Hunter leashed (or attempted — spent whether or not the save succeeds).

limitations.otherLimitations: Must have previously encountered a Hound, Hunter, or similar before selecting this Talent. Each controlled Hound reduces the user's attacks per melee by one. Unconsciousness or moving beyond range breaks the Leash.

ranges.summary: 10 feet (3 m) + 2 feet (0.6 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute; extendable at 5 P.P.E. per leashed target per additional minute.

save.summary: Target Hound or Hunter saves vs magic (15+). Failure: temporarily enslaved to verbal commands.

notes: Hound Masters are impervious and become angry when control over their minions is stolen.

name: Hyper-Awareness
description: Greatly increases awareness and observational capacity. Upon activation, the character's eyes glow faintly and ears enlarge slightly. Provides +3 to all Perception Rolls, +1 to dodge, and +10% to awareness skills such as Detect Ambush, Detect Concealment, Seduction, Surveillance Systems/Tailing, Tracking, and similar.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. to activate; five P.P.E. for each additional minute, as desired.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One minute per 5 P.P.E. spent.

notes: +1 to dodge. +10% to awareness-based skills.

name: Ignorance is Bliss
description: Tricks mortal witnesses into seeing and remembering what they want to believe rather than supernatural truth — useful when loved ones or bystanders witness transformations or monster battles. Only affects those who do not believe or do not want to believe in the paranormal; supernatural beings, psychics, magic users, and experienced believers are unaffected. Skeptics who want the truth may save vs magic (15+); disbelievers auto-succumb with permanent false memories. Can cover recent events retroactively or run preemptively when trouble is expected.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only disguises the supernatural from those who do not want to believe it exists. Effects are permanent false memories for those who succumb.

ranges.summary: 50 foot (15.2 m) radius per level of experience; cast up to 60 feet (18.3 m) away per level.

areaOfEffect.summary: 50 foot (15.2 m) radius per level; center may be up to 60 feet (18.3 m) away per level.

duration.summary: Retroactive: covers events up to 2 minutes per level before activation (witnesses still in area). Preemptive: stays active 2 minutes per level; entrants in range are affected.

save.summary: Save vs magic (15+) to see through the enchantment if the witness suspects something is wrong and wants the truth. Those who do not want to believe auto-succumb without rolling.

name: Infectious Control
description: This often-repulsive Talent allows the Nightbane to take over a person by 'infecting' him with a portion of his body. While in control, the Nightbane can see, hear and experience everything his victim does while he acts as puppeteer, controlling every word and movement of the victim. The Nightbane has to split his concentration between his own body and that of his victim.

enhancement.summary: 20 P.P.E. every additional half hour. Memory search costs 5 P.P.E. per piece of information.

duration.summary: Controlled for 30 minutes. Victims who have an M.E. of 7 or less are controlled for an hour at a time.

activationTime.summary: Usually takes one melee round from the time the piece of the Nightbane has entered the body of the victim.

penaltiesOnCaster.notes: Attacks/actions are divided evenly between the two bodies, no initiative bonus, and combat bonuses and skill performance are reduced by half unless control is relinquished.

save.summary: Save vs magic at the end of the first round to resist control. New saves allowed every 10 minutes.
Save vs psionics to resist the intrusion of a memory search.

name: Inferno Fist
description: One appendage becomes wreathed in flame, adding 2D6 damage to punches. Allows parrying magic weapons and energy weapons with the fiery hand (+1 to parry conventional melee weapons with the bare fiery hand; -3 to parry an incoming energy blast). Upon a successful strike or parry, Inferno Fist is used up and must be reactivated. Will not work with a held weapon, though objects in the flaming hand are not damaged.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. per activation for one melee round (15 seconds).

limitations.otherLimitations: Will not work in conjunction with a weapon of any sort. Inferno Fist discharges after one successful strike or parry.

ranges.summary: Self — melee combat / arm's length.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds); extinguishes if unused or if the Nightbane is knocked unconscious.

notes: If not used by end of the melee round or if knocked unconscious, the fire extinguishes.

name: Iron Will
description: Strengthens the mind's natural defenses for Nightbane without psychic abilities. +3 to save vs mind control, psionic attacks, illusions, and pain; +4 to save vs possession. Negates skill performance penalties for being under duress or under fire. The user knows when psionic powers are being used against him and that something is trying to breach his mental defenses.

ppe.notes: One melee round (15 seconds) per level of experience per activation.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) per level of experience.

notes: Negates skill performance penalties for being under duress or under fire.

name: Jam Senses
description: Counters Hounds and similar Nightlands hunters by fouling their supernatural senses. Any targeted Hound that fails a save vs magic is temporarily robbed of its ability to sense and track the supernatural and its immunity to illusions and concealment powers. Affected creatures are also slightly disoriented (-1 initiative, strike, parry, and dodge). They can still hunt by sight, scent, and skill.

ppe.notes: Three P.P.E. per enemy targeted. P.P.E. is spent whether the creature fails to save or succeeds.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must have previously encountered a Hound, Hunter, or similar before selecting this Talent. One target may be affected per level of experience; activation cost is paid for each target. Only affects supernatural senses — not sight, scent, or conventional tracking skill.

ranges.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute per level of the Nightbane's experience.

save.summary: Save vs magic (16+). Failure: loses supernatural tracking, illusion/concealment immunity, and suffers -1 initiative, strike, parry, and dodge.

savingThrow.consequenceOfFailure: Temporarily robbed of supernatural tracking senses, immunity to illusions/concealment, and suffers -1 to initiative, strike, parry, and dodge.

name: Killing Spree
description: While active, half the remaining P.P.E. of any being the user kills is stolen and temporarily added to their own pool for the duration. Stolen P.P.E. is immediately available to fuel Talents and spells. Absorbing kill-P.P.E. can trigger a murderous frenzy: for every 50 P.P.E. absorbed within one hour, cumulative 10% chance of a mental endurance save (15+) or the Nightbane loses control. Frenzy rules vary by alignment; good/selfish characters rarely harm non-combatants, evil characters may slaughter indiscriminately.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per melee round (15 seconds). Normally lasts one melee round; during frenzy, P.P.E. keeps burning to sustain Killing Spree until stolen P.P.E. is exhausted, 20 minutes pass without victims, the user is subdued for 20 minutes, or the user is slain.

limitations.otherLimitations: User must deliver the killing blow to absorb P.P.E. — not merely participate in the death. During frenzy: cannot share P.P.E., resists siphoning (+1 save), cannot draw from ley lines/nexuses, cannot cast spells, no ranged strike bonuses, offensive Talents only.

ranges.summary: Self for the ability; touch (killing blow) to absorb victim P.P.E.

duration.summary: Normally one melee round per activation; extends automatically during frenzy until end conditions are met.

notes: Frenzy bonuses/penalties: +2 initiative, +2 strike, +1 entangle, +2 save vs Horror Factor; -4 Perception, -3 parry/dodge/disarm/roll, -35% skills (except hunting/killing skills), immune to pain, will not pull punches.

name: Launch
description: Hurls a single visible inanimate object at a visible target in a telekinesis-like fashion (+2 to strike; no other strike bonuses). Weapon projectiles inflict normal damage +1D6. Objects over 30 lbs have a 33% chance to knock down human-sized opponents (lose initiative and one attack). Requires a gesture — arm wave or nod/glance toward the target.

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. per melee round. Each Launch counts as one of the user's melee attacks.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one object hurled at a time. Object and target must be visible. No fine manipulation, suspended hold, or parrying with launched objects. Objects under 20 lbs (9 kg) may be parried at -2; larger must be dodged at -1.

ranges.summary: 50 feet (15.2 m) + 20 feet (6.1 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round per activation; each Launch uses one melee attack.

statusEffects.summary: 33% chance of knocking down a human-sized opponent if weight exceeds 30 lbs. Target loses initiative and one attack.

name: Leap
description: Propels the user beyond normal jumping ability. Each P.P.E. spent adds 5 feet (1.5 m) to vertical jump height or 10 feet (3 m) to horizontal distance. Combined with a flying tackle or leap kick, adds 1D6 S.D.C. damage. May be used to dodge by leaping aside (usual dodge bonuses apply). Grants Judge Distance 60% + 3% per level for difficult jumps (moving targets or distances over 50 feet).

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. per Talent-assisted leap. Each 1 P.P.E. adds 5 ft vertical or 10 ft horizontal beyond normal jump limits. Maximum 4 P.P.E. per level of experience per activation.

limitations.otherLimitations: When attacking via Leap: -1 to strike per 20 feet (6.1 m) covered and costs an additional attack. Distance and P.P.E. cost are chosen at activation and cannot be altered mid-leap.

ranges.summary: +5 feet (1.5 m) vertical or +10 feet (3 m) horizontal per P.P.E. spent (plus normal jump), up to 4 P.P.E. per level.

duration.summary: Instant.

jumpDistanceScaling.summary: 1 P.P.E. = +5 ft (1.5 m) vertical or +10 ft (3 m) horizontal beyond the character's normal jump.

name: Leave No Trace
description: Supernatural counter to modern forensic detection. Basic mode prevents trace evidence (hairs, fingerprints, blood droplets) and eliminates tracks or footprints. Boosted mode greatly obscures the user to electronic cameras, sensors, and listening devices — blurry security footage, garbled wire taps, and laser trip wires/motion detectors/microwave fences triggering only 01–30% of the time.

activationTiers.summary: Basic: no trace evidence, no tracks or footprints — 6 P.P.E.
Boosted: basic effects plus electronic/camera/sensor obscurement — 10 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: Double duration in the Nightlands.

self.notes: Basic or boosted on self in Facade or Morphus.

others.notes: Basic benefits only; boosted Leave No Trace cannot be used on another individual.

ranges.summary: Self or other by touch.

duration.summary: Five minutes per level of the user's experience (doubled in the Nightlands).

name: Lightning Rider
description: The Nightbane becomes a being of pure electrical energy! While in electrical form, the Nightbane can travel through conductive materials at enormous speeds. The Nightbane appears to become a small pool of sparks, or a small, crackling lightning ball if no conductive material is around. The Nightbane can hitch rides on modem calls or travel through telephone lines.

enhancement.summary: The Nightbane can increase the voltage to inflict damage: 1D6 S.D.C. per 5 P.P.E. per each attack.

duration.summary: 10 P.P.E. per minute

damageModifiersNote: 8 points of damage will fry most electronics. Specially shielded systems survive 40 points of damage.

name: Livewire
description: Commands a length of cable or wire like a serpent — ripping free from devices, walls, or telephone poles and writhing through the air at speed 36 (23 mph/37 kph). The wire has two attacks per melee and is +3 to strike, entangle, and dodge. If severed or destroyed, the enchantment ends. Alternatively, the user controls it by mind and gesture, but each attack counts as one of their own melee attacks.

activationTiers.summary: Light/residential cable: 5 P.P.E. — ~6 S.D.C./foot, 1D6 whip, shock 1D6+2 once (no stun), break free P.S. 18 (2 attacks)
High-gauge cable (power lines, tow cables, etc.): 8 P.P.E. — ~20 S.D.C./foot, 2D6 whip, shock 2D6+4 (01–33% stun), break free P.S. 30 (2 attacks)
Heavy-duty cable (transmission lines, mooring cable, etc.): 12 P.P.E. — ~50 S.D.C./foot, 3D6 whip, shock +1D6 (01–66% stun 2D4 melees), break free P.S. 40 (3 attacks)

limitations.otherLimitations: Must clearly see both wire and target, or know each location with certainty. Stun chance and duration halved against supernatural opponents. Save vs electricity (13+, P.E. applies) halves shock damage and prevents stun. Damage not increased vs Nightbane-vulnerable targets. Untangling while animated is impossible — break free or cut loose only.

ranges.summary: Animates a 20 foot (6.1 m) length of wire/cable up to 100 feet (30.5 m) away.

duration.summary: 30 seconds (2 melee rounds) per level of experience.

save.summary: Shock victims save vs electricity (13+, P.E. bonus applies). Success: half damage, no stun. Failure: full damage and stunned (-2 attacks that round, -3 all combat rolls for 1D4 melee rounds; heavy cable may stun 2D4 melees).

name: Mage Siphon
description: Attempts to steal P.P.E. from a visible target within range. One P.P.E. channeled into activation steals 1D6 P.P.E. (two points steals 2D6, and so on). Stolen P.P.E. is immediately usable but can only be held for five minutes per level of experience. Magic or psionic barriers (Magic Armor, Shadow Shield, etc.) block siphoning and instead take damage equal to the P.P.E. that would have been stolen. Metal plate mail and similar spell-casting interference halves P.P.E. stolen.

enhancement.summary: One P.P.E. spent steals 1D6 P.P.E. from the target (up to the per-level cap).

ppe.notes: Maximum 2 P.P.E. spent per level of experience per use (e.g. 2 P.P.E. at level 1 = up to 2D6 stolen).

limitations.otherLimitations: Target must be visible and within range. Barriers absorb siphon damage instead (no save). Spell-casting armor halves amount stolen.

ranges.summary: 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: Instant siphon; stolen P.P.E. retained up to five minutes per level of experience.

save.summary: Save vs magic (14+). Success prevents P.P.E. from being siphoned. Does not apply when a barrier intercepts the siphon.

vampiricResourceRecovery.triggerSummary: On successful activation when the target fails their save and no barrier blocks the siphon.

name: Mass Transit
description: Teleports the user between Earth and the Nightlands (and vice versa), bringing people and/or supplies along. Highly sought by Factions for troop and supply movements. The user must travel with everyone/everything transported, know the exact destination, and may only reach the corresponding point in the other dimension or a Nightlands location previously visited. Can grab specific people and objects within the radius of influence.

enhancement.summary: Ten P.P.E. per additional individual or equivalent object (satchel, trunk, crate, or container up to 400 lbs / 180 kg).

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. transports self; +10 P.P.E. per additional passenger or cargo item.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only the corresponding point in the other dimension or a previously visited Nightlands location. User must accompany all passengers/cargo and know the exact destination. P.P.E. is expended even if unwilling targets resist and remain behind.

ranges.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius at 7th level, +10 feet (3 m) radius per level beyond 7th.

areaOfEffect.summary: 20 foot radius at 7th level (+10 feet per level above 7).

duration.summary: Must concentrate one melee round (15 seconds) per 10 P.P.E. expended before the dimensional jump occurs.

save.summary: Unwilling passengers/targets save vs magic (12+). Success avoids being taken; P.P.E. is still spent.

notes: Activation requires concentration before the jump — one melee round per 10 P.P.E. spent.

name: Mimic Spell
description: Reactivates the short-lived metaphysical echo of a recently observed spell, capturing and copying it at the same level it was originally cast. The Nightbane may direct it at a new target and adjust damage within the spell's usual parameters — useful for Sorcerers and Mystics to recast favorites quickly or use spells they do not know. Cannot mimic rituals or component-dependent spells unless the user has the required components. When multiple spells were cast in the same area, only the last spell can be mimicked.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate plus the mimicked spell's usual P.P.E. cost (e.g. a 4 P.P.E. spell costs 14 total; a 25 P.P.E. spell costs 35 total).

limitations.otherLimitations: Magic-using Nightbane may select at 2nd level; all others require 4th level. Spell trace must have been cast within the last six minutes (trace fades entirely within 1D4×10 minutes). Captured spell must be cast within one melee round (15 seconds) or the mimic is lost.

ranges.summary: May recreate a spell cast within 200 feet (61 m).

duration.summary: Mimicked spell must be cast within one melee round. Mimicked spell duration matches the original unless deliberately altered.

notes: Range, duration, damage, and effects of the reactivated spell match the original observed casting.

name: Mimic Talent
description: Duplicates the effects of almost any Nightbane Talent witnessed within 100 feet — one activation only, fully under the mimicker's control (e.g. a mimicked Doorway need not open to the same exit unless chosen). Must meet all requirements of the copied Talent (level gates, Morphus restrictions, HP/P.E. costs, etc.). Power level and capabilities match the observed user, not the mimicker's level. Duration of the mimicked Talent is halved.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate Mimic Talent plus the usual P.P.E. costs required to use the Talent being mimicked.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only duplicate the last Talent witnessed with one's own eyes. Must mimic within 60 seconds (four melee rounds) of witnessing it. Mimicked Talent is used once; cannot duplicate again unless witnessed and mimicked anew.

ranges.summary: Must witness the original Talent within 100 feet (30.5 m); line of sight required. Mimicked Talent range is roughly what was observed.

duration.summary: Mimicked Talent is instantly available; its own duration is half that of the original.

name: Mirror Search
description: The Nightbane is able to see through the mirror closest to the person or object he seeks. This does not mean that the nearest mirror will reveal the target of the search, but the closest area and mirror to it.

enhancement.summary: +5 P.P.E. per additional 10 miles (16 km).

areaOfEffect.summary: Effectively scans a 10 mile (16 km) radius.

name: Mirror Sense
description: Senses mirrors in the vicinity connected to the Nightlands with pinpoint precision — how many, their direction, and distance. Concentrating on a single mirror reveals whether it links to the Nightlands and shows a blurry, shadowy image of the Nightlands side (a shadowy reflection of the room the user stands in, not the surrounding Nightlands). Any Nightbane can tell by looking at a mirror whether it connects; this Talent senses them across a wide area before approaching.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only detects mirrors with a Nightlands counterpart — not other dimensional portals, breaches, or anomalies. Identifies which buildings contain linked mirrors; occupants are not necessarily hostile.

ranges.summary: 500 foot (152.4 m) radius + 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Five minutes per level of the Nightbane.

name: Mirror Shield
description: Summons a mirrored protective shield (buckler to medium size) strong as steel with 75 S.D.C. (damaged only if targeted). Provides +3 to parry — no other parry bonuses apply except from a high P.P. attribute. Negates the normal penalty to parry projectiles, allowing parries against bullets, magical fireballs, and energy blasts. Parried attacks may strike nearby people or objects for half damage.

limitations.otherLimitations: Shield remains on the user's arm or in hand for the duration — cannot be dropped or passed to another, but may be canceled at any time.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: Ten minutes per level of experience or until the shield is destroyed.

createdObjects.name: Mirrored Shield
createdObjects.damageRules: Only takes damage if specifically targeted. Can parry energy and magical projectile attacks without normal penalties. Parried attacks may hit nearby objects/people for half damage.

parry.summary: +3 to parry while using the shield.

name: Mirror Sight
description: Enables the Nightbane to see the other side of a mirror, into the Nightlands, and vice versa, as if it were a window. A mirror or any highly reflective surface (glass, polished metal, a quiet body of water) must be available. The character can only see what the mirror image faces in the Nightlands, and vice versa.

limitations.otherLimitations: Requires a mirror.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of the character's experience.

name: Nega
description: Counters and terminates any spell, Talent, or other supernatural ability fueled by P.P.E. that is directed at the Nightbane — including area effects, though only the user is spared. Does not work against true psionic attacks (I.S.P.). Once active, affecting powers are nullified; the user must spend P.P.E. matching the incoming power's cost or it resolves normally.

baseActivation.relativeToSummary: Spend the same P.P.E. as the spell or Talent directed at the user.

ppe.notes: Can protect up to two others by touch at once for the P.P.E. cost of one (or self plus one other). Automatically lapses when the user reaches 0 P.P.E.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot be selected until 4th level. Only negates abilities fueled by psychic or magic energy (P.P.E., etc.) — not true psionic attacks (I.S.P.). The user cannot choose what is countered; this includes their own active powers. If the user lacks enough P.P.E. to match the incoming power, it works normally.

ranges.summary: Self; or expanded to a maximum of 2 feet (0.6 m) from the user at 4th level, plus 2 feet (0.6 m) at levels 6, 8, 10, 12, and 14. Up to two others may be protected by touch.

duration.summary: Determined by the activation cost paid; automatically lapses when the user reaches 0 P.P.E.

notes: Seekers and Nocturnes often assign a Nega user to teams hunting mages or powerful supernatural beings.

name: Nightbringer
description: Absorbs light around the Nightbane, creating an area of darkness. The power also absorbs energy attacks, except Shadow Blast, Darkwhip and other darkness-based attacks and magic.

enhancement.summary: Each additional 2 P.P.E. increases the diameter of the darkness by 10 feet (3 m).

areaOfEffect.summary: 10 feet (3 m) in diameter per every 2 P.P.E. spent.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Penalties are halved for creatures with nightvision.

name: Open Channel
description: Opens to the supernatural to sense one chosen species of being in the area — while simultaneously alerting every member of that species to the Nightbane's presence.

limitations.otherLimitations: Unavailable until 2nd level. Does not indicate exact location or numbers — only rough groupings (one or two, a few [3–6], several [7–16], many [17+], great many [50+]). 'Minions of the Nightlords' or 'creatures of the Nightlands' is too broad; choose a specific species (Hounds, Hunters, Guardians, Wampyrs, Namtars, a Night Prince, etc.). Nightbane in Morphus (not Facade) can also be sensed. Each sensed individual also senses the user. To sense a different species, must reactivate. The user may restrict sensing to a 100 foot (30.5 m) radius or expand in 100 foot (30.5 m) increments up to full range.

ranges.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience; may be restricted to 100 foot increments.

duration.summary: One minute per level of experience

notes: A gut-feeling sense for the user; a dead giveaway to beings like Hounds and Hunters that a Nightbane is nearby.

name: Phantom Limb
description: Summons translucent, ghostly appendages (extra arms, claws, tentacles, etc.) that function as additional limbs — useful for combat, carrying loads, repairs, and climbing.

enhancement.summary: Summon additional phantom limbs.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per phantom limb activated. One limb per 3 levels of experience (1 at 3rd, 2 at 6th, 3 at 9th, 4 at 12th, 5 at 15th).

limitations.otherLimitations: One limb per 3 levels of experience (1 at 3rd, 2 at 6th, 3 at 9th, 4 at 12th, 5 at 15th). If meant for complex tasks like operating a computer or shooting, limbs must have digits and an opposable thumb.

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience

stepScaling.summary: +1 to parry and entangle per summoned limb.

createdObjects.name: Phantom Limb
createdObjects.damageRules: Has Supernatural P.S. of 16. Normal hand to hand strikes deal 1D6 damage.

name: Piece Work
description: By using this power, the Nightbane can remove a part of his own flesh; some actually remove an eye, ear, or other sensory body parts, but a finger or toe will do just as well. The body part will be able to hear, see and feel everything around it.

enhancement.summary: Maintenance cost per hour missing.

limitations.otherLimitations: The Nightbane cannot learn anything from the detached appendage until he reattaches it.

createdObjects.targetSummary: Detached sensory body part or appendage
createdObjects.notes: Each piece has 10 S.D.C. and 4 Hit Points (these cannot be regenerated until the piece is destroyed or reattached).

name: Pipe Bomb
description: Coalesces dark energy into a form-fitting shell resembling Shadow Shield. Absorbs incoming damage into the shell's S.D.C.; on depletion or command it explodes outward in a 15 foot (4.6 m) sphere — only the wearer is unscathed.

enhancement.summary: Each 2 P.P.E. adds 10 S.D.C. to the shell and +1D6+1 explosive damage.

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. = 10 S.D.C. and 1D6+1 damage; four = 20 S.D.C. and 2D6+2; eight = 40 S.D.C. and 4D6+4; twelve = 60 S.D.C. and 6D6+6. Total spend capped at 4 P.P.E. per level of experience.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot select blast targets — everyone and everything in the blast radius takes damage. Intentionally detonating after activation counts as two separate melee attacks. If duration ends without detonation, the shell crumbles away without exploding. Can be cancelled at any time. Only other Nightbane can tell Pipe Bomb apart from Shadow Shield.

ranges.summary: Self (15 foot / 4.6 m blast radius on detonation).

areaOfEffect.summary: 15 foot (4.6 m) blast radius on detonation.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience, until S.D.C. is depleted, willfully detonated, or cancelled.

createdObjects.name: Pipe Bomb Shield

offensiveDamageScalingByPpeStep.stepDefinition: Each 2 P.P.E. adds 10 S.D.C. to the shell and +1D6+1 to the explosive blast.

createdObjects.damageRules: Incoming damage is absorbed by the shell's S.D.C. first. On detonation, only the wearer is unscathed; blast damage is not reduced by prior shell damage.

name: Pocket Change
description: Conjures physical currency out of thin air by picturing the amount required — coins or paper notes that pass any forgery inspection, but vanish when the duration ends.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per dollar (or local equivalent) created. The Nightbane chooses coin or note form up to the P.P.E. spent.

limitations.otherLimitations: Must picture the currency to create and be familiar with its denominations and appearance — an example to focus on is best. Money vanishes completely when the duration ends.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience, then vanishes completely.

name: Premonition
description: Gives the character feelings and visions about the future, often at unexpected times or at moments of crisis. These feelings will give the character a feeling that something good, bad or dangerous is going to happen soon.

ppe.notes: 2 P.P.E. to activate actively, and 2 P.P.E. when a spontaneous occurrence happens.

name: Raven Wings
description: Manifests large black wings of solid shadow (or a winged flying apparatus) for magically powered flight. The wings have no physical substance, do not impair movement, and cannot be damaged.

enhancement.summary: Extend sustained flight.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. for 10 minutes of flight; four P.P.E. per each additional 10 minutes sustained.

limitations.otherLimitations: No altitude limit, but air gets too thin to breathe around 20,000 feet (6,096 m). Flight speed limited to 20 mph (32 kph) per level of experience. No piloting skill required; no special combat bonuses other than +2 to dodge while in flight.

ranges.summary: Self.

duration.summary: 10 minutes base; +10 minutes per 4 P.P.E. spent after activation.

name: Reality Mooring
description: Anchors incorporeal beings to the physical plane — forcing spirits, ghosts, Entities, and Astral Beings to manifest, flee, or become vulnerable to physical harm within its area of effect.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per melee round of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot be selected before 4th level. Incorporeal beings must save vs magic (16+) or manifest physically or leave for 1D6 days. Astral Projectors are snapped back to their bodies and cannot Astral Project for 24 hours. Ghosts and Entities incapable of manifesting are forced outside the area and cannot return for 1D6 days. Characters within the area cannot Astral Project or become intangible for the duration. On the Astral Plane, only Astral Travelers are forced back. Forced physical manifestation has Horror Factor 15; affected beings suffer -2 Perception, -2 initiative, -1 attack, -2 to all combat rolls, and -20% to skills for 1D4 melee rounds. Use with caution — survivors may lash out in fear.

ranges.summary: 15 feet (4.6 m) radius per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds).

save.summary: 16 or better to save vs magic; failure forces manifestation on the physical plane or banishment for 1D6 days.

statusEffects.summary: Incorporeal beings that fail the save are forced to become physical or retreat to their home plane. If forced physical, they suffer penalties.

name: Reshape Facade
description: Nightbane with this Talent can temporarily alter the shape of the Facade, changing the features, hair color and even the size of their human form. Imitating a particular person is possible, but very difficult to get right.

enhancement.summary: 4 P.P.E. per additional hour the disguise is desired.

name: See Truth
description: Every time this power is activated, the Nightbane is able to see the true nature of one person or object. This includes actually seeing a Nightbane's Facade and Morphus forms, superimposed one over the other. The alignment of the target can also be seen. It also reveals strong emanations of magic, the undead/vampires, serious illness, and strong emotions.

enhancement.summary: Every additional 4 P.P.E. raises the save number by +1 (maximum of 18, for 24 P.P.E.).

ranges.summary: Line of sight up to 300 feet (91 m) away.

save.summary: Save against magic (12 or higher) to resist revealing the truth.

name: Severance
description: Temporarily shut down his supernatural aura, making himself undetectable to other Nightbane, psychics, mages and supernatural creatures who can sense and track the supernatural aura. It makes the character's aura appear to be that of an ordinary human. While suppressed, the 'Bane cannot use any other Talents or cast magic.

limitations.otherLimitations: Duration: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience. Range: Self, only. Cuts off the user from casting magic or using other Talents while active.

name: Shadow Blast
description: Fires a black energy bolt that does 1D4 S.D.C. for every point of P.P.E. spent on it.

enhancement.summary: 1 P.P.E. per every 1D4 points of damage.

ranges.summary: 500 feet/152 m

damageModifiersNote: Damage is 1D4 S.D.C. per 1 P.P.E. spent.

name: Shadow Pockets
description: Access a mini-dimension suitable for storing small objects. Objects are held in a state of limbo and can remain indefinitely.

enhancement.summary: 8 P.P.E. to place or retrieve one object.

limitations.otherLimitations: Holds up to 5 lbs (2.25 kg) per level. No single object over 15 lbs (6.75 kg). Takes one melee attack/action to place or retrieve. If killed in Morphus, objects drop. If killed in Facade, objects are lost forever.

name: Shadow Shield
description: Creates a force field of dark energy around the Nightbane. The Shadow Shield completely surrounds the character, protecting him from toxic gases, disease, punches, bullets, heat, fire, and similar dangers as long as it remains in force; it is the shield that takes the damage inflicted by such attacks.

enhancement.summary: 2 P.P.E. for each additional 20 S.D.C.

maxEnhancementPpePerActivation.notes: Maximum of 4 P.P.E. spent per level of experience, granting a maximum of 40 S.D.C. per level.

duration.summary: 30 minutes per level of experience or until all the S.D.C. of the shadow shield is used up.

name: Shadow Slide
description: The character becomes an insubstantial shadow. While in this two-dimensional form, the Nightbane can slither along walls or across the ground, virtually invisible and able to slide through any opening, no matter how narrow.

enhancement.summary: 10 P.P.E. per each minute the Talent is maintained. Objects can be carried at an additional 2 P.P.E. per pound (0.45 kg).

name: Shadow Weapon
description: Create a melee weapon made of dark energy. Must be a one-piece melee weapon chosen when the Talent is acquired. The weapon is substantial, can parry, but disappears if it leaves the Nightbane's hands.

enhancement.summary: 5 P.P.E. for each subsequent melee round of use.

opponentConditionalModifiers.notes: Does 50% greater damage to Night Princes and Nightlords!

name: Sharing the Flesh
description: The Nightbane touches an injured person to magically transfer the other person's wounds, injury and pain to himself. The person being healed falls into a coma or sleep that lasts 1D4 melee rounds and then awakens completely well. The Nightbane can transfer as much damage as he or she can tolerate. This is a painful sacrifice.

activationTiers.name: Critically injured healing
activationTiers.effectDescription: Healing critically injured/comatose characters requires temporarily expending 24 P.P.E. and 100 S.D.C./Hit Points to stabilize the person and heal them to 2D6 Hit Points. The Nightbane falls into a coma for 2D4x10 minutes.

limitations.otherLimitations: The Nightbane must perform this act of sacrifice of his own free will.

ranges.summary: Touch

healing.summary: Transfers damage to the Nightbane, fully healing the target. Critically injured targets are brought to 2D6 Hit Points.

name: Skill Stealer
description: Temporarily robs a sentient target of a single chosen skill and transfers that knowledge to the Nightbane at the victim's skill percentage.

cannotAffect: Animals or monsters with instincts and innate abilities
limitations.otherLimitations: Unavailable until 3rd level. Only one skill per use. A newly stolen skill replaces any previously stolen skill still active. Works on physical skills and Weapon Proficiencies but grants only the skill knowledge — not associated combat bonuses (strike, parry, dodge, extra attacks, etc.); the Nightbane's P.P. and other attribute bonuses may still apply. Can target a skill the Nightbane already knows to deny the enemy its use or borrow a higher percentage from the victim.

ranges.summary: Touch or up to 40 feet (12.2 m) away; intended victim must be visible.

duration.summary: 10 minutes per level of experience; may be returned early at any time.

save.summary: Save vs magic 14 or better to resist having the skill stolen.

effectsOnSuccessfulSave.notes: Target retains their skill and the Nightbane gains nothing.

notes: Uses the victim's exact skill percentage. If the Nightbane already knows the skill at a higher percentage, the stolen version is ignored and the user's own level applies.

name: Sky Writer
description: Manipulates clouds to write sky messages visible within 5 miles (8 km), shift cloud cover, or tint and darken clouds for dramatic effect — conjuring thin clouds on clear days if needed.

activationTiers.summary: Write in the sky: 1 P.P.E. per 6 letters or symbols.
Darken or tint clouds within range: 10 P.P.E.

ppe.notes: Double all P.P.E. costs if clouds must be conjured rather than manipulated from existing ones.

limitations.otherLimitations: Tinting and darkening create dramatic storm-like appearance only — no actual weather change.

ranges.summary: Any cloud within a 5 mile (8 km) radius; sky writing visible to everyone in that radius.

duration.summary: Two minutes (8 melee rounds) per level of experience.

name: Solar Flare
description: Summons a small orb of magical sunlight usable as a 5 foot (1.5 m) lantern, a laser-like bolt (+3 to strike), or an aerial flash against creatures vulnerable to sunlight.

activationTiers.summary: Laser bolt at one target: 5D6 damage (double vs vampires and Shadow creatures); flare vanishes on impact.
Aerial sunlight flash: 2D6 damage per 4 P.P.E. spent to all sunlight-vulnerable creatures within 100 feet (30.5 m) of the user.

ppe.notes: Maximum 4 P.P.E. per level of experience per activation. Lantern mode keeps sunlight-vulnerable creatures out of the 5 foot radius but does not harm them. Aerial flash: no damage to targets shielded indoors or in darkness; Horror Factor 12 to sunlight-vulnerable creatures at night; visible 2D4 miles (3.2–12.8 km); +2 initiative for user and allies first melee while others are surprised.

limitations.otherLimitations: Not available for selection until 3rd level. Only 4 P.P.E. per level of experience may be spent per activation.
